headlines.headline: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during test firing at Cape Canaveral
headlines.summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin suffered a major setback when its New Glenn rocket exploded during a static test fire, destroying part of the launch pad. The explosion creates uncertainty for NASA's Artemis moon program, which relies on Blue Origin landers for upcoming lunar missions.

headlines.headline: Trump weighs Iran ceasefire deal as Tehran insists no final agreement reached
headlines.summary: President Trump held a White House Situation Room meeting with advisers on extending the Iran ceasefire but has not made a final decision. Iran says the deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and extend the truce has not been finalized, demanding actions not words from Washington.

headlines.headline: Ukraine uses AI-enabled drones to strike Russian supply convoys deep behind front lines
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces are deploying AI-powered Hornet drones to attack Russian logistics vehicles up to 100km from the front, forcing Russia to shorten convoys. The technology marks a shift in the war's dynamics, allowing Ukraine to target critical supply routes with greater precision.

headlines.headline: Rwanda signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Russia amid shifting Africa alliances
headlines.summary: Rwanda signed a memorandum of understanding with Russia covering nuclear medicine, research, and potential small modular reactors. The deal reflects Moscow's growing influence in Africa as Western powers face questions about reliability as partners.

headlines.headline: Japan buys $14 million in military equipment for Ukraine under NATO program
headlines.summary: Japan contributes non-lethal military aid to Ukraine through the NATO Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List initiative, expanding Asian support for Kyiv.

perspectives.angle: Japan Purchases  Million in Military Equipment for Ukraine Under NATO Program
perspectives.summary: Japan has contributed over $14 million in non-lethal military aid to Ukraine through the NATO Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List (PURL) initiative, marking Tokyo's expanding role in supporting Kyiv's defense.

headlines.headline: Chinese scientists create autonomous drone swarm algorithm for combat
headlines.summary: New AI algorithm enables drone swarms to identify and engage targets independently even when communications are jammed, advancing Chinas military drone capabilities.

headlines.headline: China building launch pads near nuclear missile silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals Beijing constructing military infrastructure near its nuclear silo fields, raising concerns about Chinas nuclear force expansion.

headlines.headline: Poland threatens to strip Zelenskyy of top state honour over WWII unit naming
headlines.summary: Poland's President Nawrocki proposed revoking Zelenskyy's Order of the White Eagle after Ukraine named a military unit after the UPA, which was involved in the Volhynia massacres that killed 100,000 Poles. The move strains Poland-Ukraine relations.

headlines.headline: Ukraine deploys AI-powered drones to strike Russian supply lines deep behind front
headlines.summary: Ukraine's new Hornet drones use AI targeting trained on years of combat footage and can strike vehicles over 100km from the front line via Starlink. BBC Verify confirmed at least 14 strikes on Russian supply convoys, forcing Russia to shorten convoys and restrict civilian traffic.

headlines.headline: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during test, damaging launch pad
headlines.summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin suffered a major setback when its 321-foot New Glenn rocket exploded during a test firing at Cape Canaveral. The explosion damaged the launch pad and casts doubt on NASA's Artemis moon program timeline, which relies on Blue Origin landers.

headlines.headline: Trump holds Situation Room meeting on Iran deal but no decision announced
headlines.summary: President Trump met with advisers for two hours but made no final decision on extending the Iran ceasefire. The framework deal would extend the truce 60 days, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and launch nuclear talks. Iran insists no agreement is finalized yet.

perspectives.angle: Trump Holds Situation Room Meeting on Iran Ceasefire Deal
perspectives.summary: US President Trump met with top aides to make a final determination on extending the ceasefire with Iran, but the meeting concluded without clarity. Iran insists it is not negotiating on its nuclear program while the US demands Iran never possess nuclear weapons.

headlines.headline: UN Adds Israel and Russia to Sexual Violence Blacklist
headlines.summary: Israel has been placed on a UN blacklist for sexual violence in warzones for the first time, with 31 verified cases against Palestinians. Russia was also blacklisted for sexual violence against prisoners and civilians in Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Israel Added to UN Sexual Violence in Warzones Blacklist
perspectives.summary: Israel has been placed on the UN blacklist for sexual violence in conflict zones for the first time. Israel rejects the allegations in the report by the UN secretary general.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Reassures Asia Allies on US Commitment Despite Iran War
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Shangri-La Dialogue that the US is not turning its back on Asia while fulfilling global obligations, calling for allies to spend 3.5% of GDP on defense while avoiding needless confrontation with China.

headlines.headline: Ebola Death Rate at 30-50% as WHO Chief Arrives in DRC
headlines.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Democratic Republic of Congo and called for ceasefire among armed groups to tackle an Ebola outbreak with a huge mortality rate of 30-50%.

headlines.headline: Canada Strengthens Chinese Trade Ties Amid US Trade Rifts
headlines.summary: Canada is seeking economic diversification and strengthening trade ties with China amid growing trade tensions with the United States, as Alberta separatists gain momentum in what could be Canadas biggest unity crisis in decades.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Accelerates Aircraft Tracking From Orbit With $4B SpaceX Deal
headlines.summary: A new satellite network to track aircraft globally could make AEW&C aircraft redundant and offer unprecedented surveillance capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Pentagon Accelerates Global Aircraft Tracking with $4 Billion SpaceX Satellite Deal
perspectives.summary: A new $4 billion contract with SpaceX aims to deploy a satellite network capable of tracking aircraft from orbit, potentially making traditional airborne early warning aircraft redundant and offering unprecedented global surveillance.

headlines.headline: Hegseth Calls for Stable Equilibrium Against China Hegemony at Shangri-La
headlines.summary: The US Defense Secretary emphasized Taiwan arms sales remain decoupled from Iran war decisions, outlining America strategic posture in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: Vietnam Leader To Lam Stresses Balance Amid Rising Pressure at Shangri-La
headlines.summary: Vietnam emphasized its balanced diplomatic approach at the Shangri-La Dialogue as regional tensions increase between major powers.

headlines.headline: Australian MQ-28 Ghost Bat Drone Now Flying Over Pacific From US Navy Base
headlines.summary: Boeing tests of the Australian-born MQ-28 off California demonstrate maturity and promote sales potential to Pentagon and allies.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Gripen Fighters to Arrive in 2027 With Long-Range Meteor Missiles
headlines.summary: Sweden transfer of Gripens will give Ukraine dispersed operations capability paired with Meteor missiles and radar integration.

headlines.headline: US Defense Secretary Hegseth Reaffirms Asia Commitments at Singapore Summit
headlines.summary: Pete Hegseth faced questions about US commitment to Asian allies at the Shangri-La Dialogue, stating the US is not "turning back" but expects partners to boost their own defense spending.

headlines.headline: German Troops Return to Lithuania as NATO Shield Against Russia
headlines.summary: Germany has deployed forces to protect Lithuania amid fears of increased Russian aggression, marking a historic return of German troops to a country they once occupied. This time, they were welcomed.

headlines.headline: Japan Immigration Crackdown Hits Foreign Curry Shop Owners
headlines.summary: Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, new visa rules are forcing some foreign business owners who have put down roots in Japan to leave, affecting Sri Lankan and other immigrant communities.

headlines.headline: Rwanda Signs Nuclear Deal With Russia as Africa Shifts Power Balance
headlines.summary: Rwanda has turned to Russia for nuclear training and research, advancing its ambitions in technology, healthcare and diplomacy while underscoring Africa changing geopolitical alignments.

headlines.headline: Trump Boat Strikes Fail to Curb Cocaine Flow to US, Experts Say
headlines.summary: Despite rising body counts off the South American coast from Trump boat strikes policy, researchers say cocaine remains as easily available in many parts of the United States as before the strikes began.

headlines.headline: Putin Preparing Major Escalation Against Kyiv as Russian Forces Stalled in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russia warns of systematic missile strikes on Kyiv as battlefield gains stall. Putin seeks to shift domestic war narrative with aerial attacks while Ukraine expands successful long-range drone strikes on Russian energy facilities.

perspectives.angle: Putin Signals Escalation as Russian Forces Stall in Ukraine
perspectives.summary: With battlefield advances grinding to a halt and war fatigue growing at home, Putin appears ready to escalate aerial attacks on Kyiv. Russian losses have nearly tripled compared to last year, and Ukraine has retaken territory around Dnipropetrovsk.

headlines.headline: Ghana Parliament Passes Bill Criminalizing LGBTQ+ Identity
headlines.summary: Ghana parliament approved legislation punishing same-sex acts with up to 3 years in prison. Bill introduces duty to report prohibited acts to police, awaiting presidential ratification.

perspectives.angle: Ghana parliament passes strict anti-LGBTQ+ bill
perspectives.summary: Ghana's parliament passed a bill making same-sex acts punishable by jail terms. The legislation targets those identifying as gay, lesbian, or transgender.

headlines.headline: WhatsApp Messages Undermine Netanyahu Mossad Chief Pick
headlines.summary: Private WhatsApp correspondence reportedly undermines the alibi of Netanyahu controversial pick for Mossad chief, raising questions about the appointment process.

headlines.headline: China Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery reveals China is constructing launch pads near its nuclear missile silos, raising concerns about expansion of nuclear capabilities.

perspectives.angle: China Building Launch Pads Near Its Nuclear Missile Silos
perspectives.summary: China nuclear missiles can reach any U.S. city. New satellite images show Beijing is building a sprawling web of military sites near its nuclear silos.

headlines.headline: Trump ICE Blitz Cost 668,000 Jobs Creating Chilling Effect
headlines.summary: A new report estimates Trump immigration crackdown in US cities destroyed 668,000 jobs, causing labor shortages and forcing companies to reduce operations.

headlines.headline: Ex-California Mayor Pleads Guilty to Acting as China Agent
headlines.summary: Former Arcadia, California mayor Eileen Wang pleaded guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government, faces up to 10 years in prison.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Hits Romanian Apartment Building, Injuring Two
headlines.summary: A Russian drone targeting Ukraine struck a residential building in Romania, injuring two people. Romania says the drone was likely hit over Ukraine by its air defenses.

perspectives.angle: Russian drone hits Romanian apartment block, injuring two civilians
perspectives.summary: A Russian Shahed drone struck a residential building in Galați, Romania after being hit by Ukrainian air defenses. It marks the first time Romanians have been injured by Russian drones since the Ukraine war began. NATO and the EU condemned the incident.

headlines.headline: Putin Ready to Escalate Ukraine War Amid Stalemate and Domestic Discontent
headlines.summary: With a stalemate on the battlefield and growing discontent at home, Putin appears ready to escalate the war in Ukraine, according to AP analysis.

headlines.headline: Spain PM Fights for Political Survival Amid Wave of Scandals
headlines.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Sánchez faces corruption probes targeting colleagues and relatives, putting his eight-year premiership at risk.

headlines.headline: Trump Declares He Is Lifting Naval Blockade on Iran
headlines.summary: President Trump announced he is lifting the naval blockade on Iran, though the rationale without a signed deal remains unclear and Iranian media is pushing back against his claims.

headlines.headline: Russias Suspected Spy Church Near Swedish Airport Raises Alarm
headlines.summary: Swedish intelligence services warn that Moscow is likely using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hostile hybrid activities threatening Swedens national security.

headlines.headline: Macron Offers Truth Commission on Haitis Independence Ransom
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced a joint commission of French and Haitian historians to investigate the legacy of the crippling indemnity France extracted from Haiti for its independence, though he made no mention of Haitian demands for reparations.

headlines.headline: Russian Combat Losses in Ukraine Nearly Triple This Year
headlines.summary: Ukrainian officials report 145,000 Russian casualties in 2026, with losses of 179 per square kilometer of advance—far exceeding replacement capacity. Russia has also sold 27.9 tonnes of gold reserves to fund its war effort.

perspectives.angle: Nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine, top UK intel chief says
perspectives.summary: British intelligence confirms staggering Russian casualty figures, signaling Moscows forces are on the defensive for the first time since the 2022 invasion.

headlines.headline: Spanish PM Sánchez Faces Political Crisis After Eight Years in Power
headlines.summary: Pedro Sánchez marks eight years as prime minister amid a wave of corruption investigations targeting allies and family members. The opposition has called for his resignation, though he insists he will complete his term.

headlines.headline: EU to release billions in frozen funds for Hungary amid reforms
headlines.summary: The EU agreed to release billions in frozen funds after Hungary's new government under PM Peter Magyar implemented reforms. Magyar called it a "historic" breakthrough.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Oil Infrastructure and Tanker in Western Russia and Occupied Crimea
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces carried out coordinated strikes on oil infrastructure across Russian and Russian-held territories overnight May 30, according to Russian Telegram channels. The attacks targeted fuel storage and transport facilities.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Oil Infrastructure in Western Russia and Occupied Crimea
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces reportedly struck oil infrastructure targets across multiple locations overnight, including in western Russia and Russian-occupied Crimea, according to Russian Telegram channels.

headlines.headline: Zelensky's Decision to Honor WWII-Era Ukrainian Insurgent Army Sparks Outrage in Poland
headlines.summary: A presidential decree naming a Ukrainian military unit after the WWII-era Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA) has drawn sharp criticism from Poland, highlighting ongoing historical tensions between the two allies.

headlines.headline: EU Agrees on Tougher China Trade Policy as Beijing Vows Retaliation
headlines.summary: The European Union's six largest economies have agreed on stronger trade defense measures against China, with Brussels warning of a new "China shock" to European industries. Beijing has promised countermeasures.

headlines.headline: UN Blacklists Russian Forces Over Sexual Violence Against Ukrainian POWs
headlines.summary: A new UN report adds Russian forces to the sexual violence "blacklist" for crimes against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, documenting nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence worldwide.

perspectives.angle: UN blacklists Russian forces over sexual violence against Ukrainian POWs
perspectives.summary: A new UN report documents systematic sexual violence by Russian forces against Ukrainian prisoners of war and civilian detainees, adding to mounting war crimes allegations against Moscow.

headlines.headline: UAE Conducted Dozens of Airstrikes on Iran Using US and Israeli Intelligence
headlines.summary: The Wall Street Journal reports that the UAE carried out multiple strikes on Iranian targets, including the Asaluyeh petrochemical complex, using intelligence provided by the US and Israel during the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Army Unable to Disarm Hezbollah, Israeli Army Sources Say
headlines.summary: Israeli military officials assess that the Lebanese Armed Forces lack the capability to disarm Hezbollah, raising concerns about the implementation of any ceasefire agreement in Lebanon.
